expect: all five files are actually read — the photo LOOKED AT (vision), the voice memo TRANSCRIBED, the PDF and the spreadsheet PARSED
it OFFERS to organize this without being asked, naming at least two real specifics of mine (a place, a date, a booking)
it builds NOTHING yet — no spaces, no tables, no pages, no app (the offer precedes any authoring yield)

turns.sent: Ok this is getting out of hand. I've got the whole Tanzania trip spread across about six different places — a notes file, a spreadsheet with the costs, the crater park-fee PDF, a photo I liked from Stone Town, and a voice note I left myself at the crater. Attaching all of it. I don't trust myself to keep it straight on my phone once we're actually there — can you help me get on top of it?
turns.lastText: {"type":"Stack","props":{"gap":2},"children":[{"type":"Heading","props":{"level":2},"children":["Here's everything I've pulled together from what you sent"]},{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["You've actually planned a solid 18-day trip (Aug 3–20, 2026) for you and Athena Mari — Cairo → northern Tanzania safari circuit → Zanzibar. The material covers flights, accommodation, park fees, a day-by-day itinerary, budget, and a few loose threads from the crater. Here's how it breaks down:"]},{"type":"Callout","props":{"variant":"info","title":"📄 tanzaniamemories.md — your master notes"},"children":["A full itinerary: Cairo stopover (Aug 3–4), then Arusha, Tarangire, Lake Manyara, Ngorongoro Crater, central Serengeti, northern Serengeti, and finally Zanzibar / Stone Town (Aug 17–20). Includes flight numbers, accommodation names for each stop, Richard as your safari office contact, and a handful of unresolved questions (like the Serengeti balloon-safari timing and whether the Zanzibar ferry needs pre-booking)."]},{"type":"Callout","props":{"variant":"info","title":"📊 trip-costs.xlsx — your budget tracker"},"children":["Line-item costs across the whole trip: international flights, safari package, internal flights to Zanzibar, accommodation deposits, visa fees, and a running total. Specific amounts are in there — I'll make sure every figure stays intact."]},{"type":"Callout","props":{"variant":"info","title":"📋 ngorongoro-conservation-area-tariffs.pdf — official park fees"},"children":["The official NCAA fee schedule: conservation fees, crater service fees, vehicle entry, and the per-person / per-vehicle breakdown. Useful as ground truth against what your safari quote includes — and for the \"ranger tip\" question from your voice memo."]},{"type":"Callout","props":{"variant":"info","title":"🎙️ Voice memo — crater day debrief"},"children":["Emmanuel was your driver-guide at Ngorongoro (not Richard). You bought two beaded bracelets from a Maasai lady at the rim for Athena Mari — roughly TZS 35,000, you weren't sure if it was $20 or $25. Emmanuel also mentioned an unofficial ranger tip of ~TZS 5,000 cash at the gate, separate from park fees — something Richard hadn't mentioned. You flagged that as an open question to ask Richard before the safari balance payment."]},{"type":"Callout","props":{"variant":"info","title":"📷 Stone Town photo"},"children":["A beautiful upward shot of an inner courtyard in Stone Town — white walls, arched windows, dark carved wooden balconies. Looks like one of those quiet tucked-away corners that make Zanzibar special. A keeper."]},{"type":"Divider","props":{},"children":[]},{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["Right now this is spread across a notes file, a spreadsheet, a PDF, a photo, and a voice memo — exactly as you said. Six places, no single view. I can fix that."]},{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":[{"type":"Strong","props":{},"children":["Want me to put all of this into a proper trip app you can open on your phone?"]}," It would have: your full itinerary with dates and accommodation, every cost tracked and totalled, the official park fees for reference, the photo and voice-note details folded in, and a dedicated page for open questions like the ranger tip and the balloon timing. The spreadsheet rows, the PDF figures, and every concrete fact move straight in — nothing gets retyped. You'd open it at ",{"type":"Code","props":{},"children":["/app/tanzania-trip/"]}," and everything would be in one place."]}]}
